extra.message: User input from params['id'][0] is concatenated directly into a SQL query string passed to cursor.execute(). An attacker can manipulate the 'id' parameter to exfiltrate all database contents, including password hashes and session tokens.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: In AuthBypass.run(), the 'password' parameter is retrieved unsanitized and concatenated directly into a SQL query: "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='" + username + "' AND password='" + password + "'". Although username is sanitized with re.sub, password is not, allowing SQL injection via the password field (e.g., password value ' OR '1'='1). This enables authentication bypass.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: In CommandInjection.run(), the 'domain' parameter from user input is concatenated into a shell command string (' '.join([command, domain])) and passed to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. No sanitization is applied. An attacker can inject arbitrary OS commands via the domain parameter (e.g., domain=google.com;cat /etc/passwd).

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: In do_BDR(), the full HTTP request path (self.path[1:]) is passed directly to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. An attacker sending a custom HTTP method BDR with any path can execute arbitrary OS commands on the server when risk >= 3.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: In UnsafeDeserialization.run(), the 'object' query parameter is base64-decoded and passed directly to pickle.loads() without any validation or integrity check. pickle.loads() on attacker-controlled data allows arbitrary Python code execution (remote code execution). The class even demonstrates this with its own RCE inner class.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: In PathTraversal.run(), the 'path' query parameter is passed to os.path.abspath() and directly opened without any directory restriction check. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the server (e.g., path=../../etc/passwd). Additionally, if '://' is present in the path, it is passed to urllib.request.urlopen(), enabling Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF).

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: In PathTraversal.run(), when the 'path' parameter contains '://', it is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen() without validation of the scheme or destination. An attacker can supply arbitrary URLs (e.g., file://, http://, ftp://) to make the server issue requests to internal services or read local files via file:// URIs, constituting SSRF.

cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

extra.message: In XSSReflected.run(), the 'msg' query parameter value is returned directly as HTML content without any HTML encoding or sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript via the msg parameter (e.g., ?msg=<script>alert(1)</script>).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: In XSSStored.run(), the 'comment' parameter is stored in the database without sanitization (line 88), and retrieved comments are rendered directly into HTML without escaping (line 97: '{}'.format(column)). An attacker can store a persistent XSS payload that executes in every visitor's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: In UnvalidatedRedirect.run(), the 'path' parameter is embedded directly into a JavaScript window.location.replace() call without validation. An attacker can supply an arbitrary external URL to redirect victims to malicious sites (e.g., ?path=https://evil.com).

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: In SessionFixation.run(), the 'path' parameter is embedded directly into a JavaScript window.location call without validation. An attacker can use this as an open redirect to arbitrary external URLs.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: In SessionFixation.run(), the 'session' query parameter is accepted and directly set as the SESSIONID cookie value without validation. An attacker can fix a victim's session ID to a known value before authentication, and then hijack the authenticated session after the victim logs in.

cwe: CWE-384: Session Fixation

extra.message: In XSRequestForgery.run(), the settings update form (email change at line 324) uses GET method with no CSRF token. Any page on any origin can cause state-changing actions by tricking the authenticated user's browser into issuing a GET request to /settings?email=attacker@evil.com.

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: In ExecutionAfterRedirect.run(), the admin page content (sensitive links for Manage Users and Update Database Settings) is returned in the response body before the JavaScript redirect to /login is appended. An attacker intercepting the raw HTTP response or using a client that ignores JavaScript redirects can access the protected admin content without authentication.

cwe: CWE-698: Execution After Redirect (EAR)

extra.message: The SSL context explicitly re-enables insecure protocols and options by unsetting OP_NO_SSLv3 (allowing SSLv3), OP_NO_COMPRESSION (allowing TLS compression, enabling CRIME attack), and OP_CIPHER_SERVER_PREFERENCE. These settings weaken TLS security significantly.

cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength

extra.message: The server explicitly sets 'X-XSS-Protection: 0' to disable the browser's built-in XSS filter, and sets 'Content-Security-Policy: default-src * \'unsafe-inline\'' which allows inline scripts from any origin. These headers intentionally remove XSS protections.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: HTTP responses lack security headers: no X-Frame-Options or Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors directive (enabling clickjacking), no Strict-Transport-Security (even when SSL is enabled), and cookies set via Set-Cookie lack the HttpOnly and Secure flags (see line 234), exposing session tokens to JavaScript access and network interception.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag

extra.message: In AuthBypass.run(), hardcoded credentials 'dsvpwa'/'dsvpwa' are checked (username == 'dsvpwa' and password == 'dsvpwa') and grant access to a built-in account that bypasses the database entirely.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: Plaintext credentials for multiple user accounts are stored in db/users.xml, including an admin password (Y3RFgwZP$v6v#D) and weak passwords (guest, babygirl, 123456). These are loaded at server startup and inserted into the in-memory database.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: In StaticHandler.find(), the path from the HTTP request is used to open files with open('./{}'.format(path)) without validating that the resolved path stays within the static/ directory. Although an attacker's path starts from the URL, traversal sequences (e.g., /../etc/passwd) could allow reading files outside the intended static directory.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: In do_GET(), exceptions are caught and the full exception message (str(ex)) is returned in the HTTP response body with a 500 status. This can expose internal stack traces, file paths, database schema details, or other sensitive information to the client.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
